request.question: Why was O.J Mayo, named the Most Valuable Player at the 2007 Anaheim Classic dismissed from the NBA?

title: Scott Niedermayer
Jerry Lucas
Simona Gioli
Russell Westbrook
2007 Anaheim Classic
2013 NBA Summer League
Leanne Del Toso
Kevin Durant
CBA Most Valuable Player
O. J. Mayo
sentences: Scott Niedermayer (born August 31, 1973) is a Canadian former ice hockey defenceman and current Special Assignment Coach of the Anaheim Ducks.
He played 18 seasons and over 1,000 games in the National Hockey League (NHL) for the New Jersey Devils and Anaheim Ducks.
Niedermayer is a four-time Stanley Cup champion and played in five NHL All-Star Games.
He won the James Norris Memorial Trophy in 2003–04 as the NHL's top defenceman and the Conn Smythe Trophy in 2007 as the most valuable player of the playoffs.
On January 27, 2017, in a ceremony during the All-Star Weekend in Los Angeles, Niedermayer was part of the second group of players to be named one of the '100 Greatest NHL Players' in history.
Jerry Ray Lucas (born March 30, 1940) is an American former basketball player and memory education expert.

He was a nationally-awarded high school player, national college star at Ohio State, and 1960 gold medal Olympian and international player before starring as a professional player in the National Basketball Association (NBA).
As a collegian, Lucas led the Ohio State Buckeyes to the 1960 college national championship and three straight NCAA finals.
He remains today the only three-time Big Ten Player of the Year, and was also twice named NCAA Player of the Year.

As a professional, Lucas was named All-NBA First Team three times, a NBA All-Star seven times, was 1964 NBA Rookie of the Year, and was named Most Valuable Player of the 1965 NBA All-Star Game among other honors and awards.
He was inducted to the Naismith Memorial Basketball Hall of Fame in 1980.
Simona Gioli (born September 17, 1977 in Rapallo) is a volleyball player from Italy.
Currently, she plays for Galatasaray in Turkey.
Gioli was a member of the Women's National Team that won the gold medal at the 2007 European Championship in Belgium and Luxembourg.
Gioli was named "Most Valuable Player and ""Best Blocker" at the 2007 FIVB Women's World Cup and 2009 FIVB Women's World Grand Champions Cup.
She was also awarded "MVP" at the 2006-07 CEV Cup won by her team Sirio Perugia.
She won the 2007–08 CEV Indesit Champions League with Sirio Perugia and also was individually awarded "Most Valuable Player".
Russell Westbrook III (born November 12, 1988) is an American professional basketball player for the Oklahoma City Thunder of the National Basketball Association (NBA).
He is a six-time NBA All-Star, and a two-time NBA All-Star Game Most Valuable Player, winning consecutive awards in 2015 and 2016.
He is also a six-time All-NBA Team member and led the league in scoring in 2014–15 and 2016–17.
In 2017, Westbrook became one of two players in NBA history to average a triple-double for a season, along with Oscar Robertson in 1962.
He also set a record for the most triple-doubles in a season, with 42.
He was subsequently named the 2016–17 NBA Most Valuable Player.
The 2007 Anaheim Classic was played between November 22 and November 25, 2007 at the Anaheim Convention Center in Anaheim, California.
The champion of the tournament was Southern California, who defeated Southern Illinois in the Championship Game.
The Most Valuable Player was O. J. Mayo of Southern California.
The 2013 NBA Summer League is a pro basketball league run by the NBA just after the 2013 NBA draft.
It gives newly drafted players a chance to test their skills against each other, and to give them a feel for professional basketball.
All 30 NBA teams participated, along with the D-League Select.
The Miami Heat were the only team to participate in both Summer Leagues.
It ran from July 7–12 in Orlando and July 12–22 in Las Vegas.
Jeremy Lamb of the Oklahoma City Thunder was named the Most Valuable Player of the Orlando Summer League.
Jonas Valančiūnas of the Toronto Raptors went on to be named the Most Valuable Player of the Las Vegas Summer League.
Ian Clark of the Golden State Warriors was named the Most Valuable Player of the Las Vegas Summer League Championship Game.
Leanne Del Toso (born 12 August 1980) is a 3.5 point wheelchair basketball player who represented Australia at the 2012 Summer Paralympics in London, where she won a silver medal.
Diagnosed with chronic inflammatory demyelinating polyneuropathy at the age of nineteen, Del Toso started playing wheelchair basketball in 2006.
Playing in the local Victorian competition, she was named the league's most valuable player in 2007.
That year started playing for the Knox Ford Raiders in the Women's National Wheelchair Basketball League (WNWBL).
The following year, she was named the team's Players' Player and Most Valuable Player (MVP).
Kevin Wayne Durant (born September 29, 1988) is an American professional basketball player for the Golden State Warriors of the National Basketball Association (NBA).

He has won an NBA championship, an NBA Most Valuable Player Award, the Bill Russell NBA Finals Most Valuable Player Award, the NBA All-Star Game Most Valuable Player Award, four NBA scoring titles, the NBA Rookie of the Year Award, and two Olympic gold medals.
Durant has also been selected to seven All-NBA teams and eight NBA All-Star teams.
The Chinese Basketball Association (CBA) MVP is the annual award that is handed out at the end of each Chinese Basketball Association (CBA) regular season to the league's most valuable player.
From the league's inaugural 1995–96 season until the 2011–12 season, only Chinese/Taiwanese players were eligible to win the award.
Since the 2012–13 season, separate awards for both domestic and international players are handed out.
Domestic players are eligible to win the CBA Domestic Most Valuable Player award, while international players are eligible to win the CBA International Most Valuable Player award.
Ovinton J'Anthony "O. J." Mayo (born November 5, 1987) is an American professional basketball player.
He played one season of college basketball for the USC Trojans while earning first-team All-Pac-10 honors.
The team forfeited all of its wins that season and Mayo lost his remaining three years of National Collegiate Athletic Association (NCAA) eligibility after it was ruled he received improper benefits.
Mayo entered the 2008 NBA draft and was selected by the Minnesota Timberwolves with the third overall pick.
He was later traded to the Memphis Grizzlies, with whom he played four seasons.
In 2011, he tested positive for a banned steroid and was suspended by the NBA for 10 games.
Mayo signed with the Dallas Mavericks in 2012, and then with the Milwaukee Bucks in 2013.
On July 1, 2016, Mayo was dismissed from the NBA for violating the league's anti-drug program.

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Role and goal: Summarize the first-hop evidence so the next agent can find the missing second-hop information. Produce a single short, factual `summary` that: (1) compresses the grounded facts in the provided passages; (2) exposes the bridge entity, the specific missing fact, or the retrieval direction needed for the next hop; and (3) gives concrete evidence anchors (quoted or clearly paraphrased phrases) that a retriever can use.

Content rules:
- Keep the summary concise and fact-focused. Do not attempt to produce the final answer; describe only what the passages support and what is still needed.
- Preserve the same passage facts and phrasing as you would otherwise (you may quote or paraphrase). Do not change which facts are exposed; only condense them.
- You may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts from the passages to state a bridge or to justify a next-hop direction. Do not invent facts, infer new temporal facts, or treat composition as unsupported when the necessary premises are present.
- If the passages lack a required premise, or contain conflicting identity links, explicitly state which premise or identity link is missing or ambiguous so the next agent can retrieve or disambiguate it.

Date-format rule (narrow formatting change):
- When the summary includes a date token that appears in the passages (any calendar date or parenthetical date taken from the text), render that date using the canonical form FullMonthName day, year (for example, "September 27, 2017") inside the evidence description. This normalization only changes the string form of the date token; it must not add, infer, or imply any additional temporal facts, nor be used as a reason to produce a final answer.
- Otherwise, leave all other wording and evidence presentation unchanged. Preserve quotations or clear paraphrases of the same passage text; only adjust the date token's string format.

Output contract:
- Return exactly one field `summary` containing the compressed first-hop evidence, the bridge/missing-fact signal, and any retrieval direction needed for the next hop. Do not output final answers, extra metadata, or new fields.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Produce exactly one compact retrieval query string (single line) in the `query` field and nothing else. The query should be a concise keyword-style search targeted to recover the missing bridge or supporting evidence needed by downstream agents.

General behavior:
- Use the visible facts in summary_1 as anchors (album titles, artist names, dates, relation terms) and compose a focused query that is likely to retrieve passages supporting the needed relation. Prefer concrete anchors and relation keywords over full sentences.
- Do not include explanatory text, user-facing prompts, or requests-for-clarification in the `query` field. Do not invent or assert missing facts (for example, do not supply an artist name that is not present in summary_1).
- Preserve the single-query output contract: emit exactly one string and no other fields or commentary.

Disambiguation for short/ambiguous title tokens:
- If an anchor in summary_1 is a short or potentially ambiguous title token, do not automatically append an entertainment-domain keyword. Only append a plain entertainment disambiguator keyword (for example: film movie album song novel author director) when the original question itself contains explicit entertainment-role words (e.g., film, movie, album, song, directed, author, novella) or when the question explicitly lists multiple title-like tokens. In those cases, include the visible anchor(s) from summary_1 plus the appropriate entertainment keyword to bias retrieval. If the question lacks entertainment cues and does not list multiple titles, do not guess or add an entertainment disambiguator—keep the query anchored to the visible tokens only.

Special-case for missing album artist or genre (narrow semantic boundary):
- If summary_1 shows an album title but does not include the album's artist or genre, or if summary_1 explicitly signals inability to determine genre or explicitly asks for the artist/passage (phrases such as "can't determine" or "please provide the artist"), then emit exactly one compact search phrase formatted as:
  <AlbumTitle> album artist genre
  Use only the visible album title as it appears in summary_1 followed by the three keywords: album artist genre. Do not add punctuation, explanatory words, alternate tokens, or candidate artist names.
- Do not apply this special-case if summary_1 already contains the artist or genre, or if the album anchor is an ambiguous short token that requires human clarification.

Fallback and downstream behavior:
- If retrieval using the emitted query returns no useful passages, the downstream agent may then ask the user for clarification or additional context. Do not ask the user inside the `query` field.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the field `summary`.

Task: Integrate the first-hop `context` with the newly retrieved `passages` and produce a single concise `summary` that preserves the facts needed by the final answer. Resolve the earlier bridge when the supplied passages support doing so; retain uncertainty only when the post-retrieval evidence is genuinely insufficient, ambiguous, or conflicting.

Guidelines:
- Treat a concise answer in `context` as the primary claim. Do not replace that concise context answer unless the `passages` contain a direct, identity-aligned contradiction.
- Before attributing any fact from a passage to the entity or relation named in `context`, confirm identity alignment (clear aliasing or explicit linking). Do not assume two similarly named mentions are the same without such alignment.
- When the context contains a concise bridge claim about a named entity and at least one passage provides an explicit, identity-aligned statement linking that same named entity (or a clearly indicated alias) to the asserted relation, append exactly one brief corroborating sentence that paraphrases that passage's linking fact and references the corroborating passage. Perform an explicit identity-alignment check (alias or explicit linking); do not treat mere name co-occurrence or a passage that merely mentions the name as sufficient. Do not append this corroboration if the `context` already contains the identical corroborating fact verbatim. Keep this addition short and keep the overall summary compact.
- If the `passages` are weak, ambiguous, or conflict with each other or with the context claim (but do not provide an explicit, identity-aligned contradiction), preserve the context claim and append a single short clause that flags the uncertainty or contradiction. When flagging, you may optionally include a verbatim quoted phrase from a passage of up to 10 words to show the source wording. Do not invent resolutions beyond what the `passages` show.
- If the `passages` contain an explicit, identity-aligned contradiction to the context claim, state that the passages contradict the context claim and briefly note the conflicting passage fact (optionally with a ≤10-word quote). Only in this case may the summary stop asserting the context claim as definitive.
- Keep the `summary` compact (aim for 1–3 sentences). Use uncertainty language only when a required premise, identity link, or relation is genuinely missing, ambiguous, or conflicting. Do not default to uncertainty when composition across context and passages licenses a conclusion.
- You may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts from `context` and `passages` to form a licensed multi-hop conclusion; the supporting facts need not all appear in a single passage.
- Do not include long verbatim quotes, do not invent facts, and do not attempt to resolve contradictions beyond reporting them as present in the `passages`.

Output: a single `summary` string that follows the rules above.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the field `answer`.

Use only the information in the provided summaries as evidence. summary_2 is the later, integrated evidence state and should be the primary source for deciding the minimal answer; summary_1 may contain useful first-hop compression or retrieval-oriented language and may be used to supply premises that, together with summary_2, license a conclusion. Do not invent facts or use outside knowledge.

Emit only the minimal answer string supported by the visible summaries in the `answer` field. Do not include explanations, justification, or any additional text.

Preserve multi-hop composition: you may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts across the summaries to form a licensed conclusion even when no single sentence states the whole relation. Distinguish licensed composition from unsupported inference; emit uncertainty only when a required premise, identity link, or relation is genuinely missing, ambiguous, or conflicting in the summaries.

Minimal-answer formatting rule (explicit):
- If the summaries jointly support one minimal literal and that literal appears verbatim in the summaries and consists of multiple contiguous tokens (for example: a full personal name, a full date, or a place name), emit that full literal exactly as it appears in the summaries (verbatim, unchanged). Do not truncate or coerce it to a single token.
- If the jointly supported minimal answer is itself a single-token label, emit exactly that single token (in lowercase) and nothing else.
- When the summaries contain both a short single-token alias and, in the later integrated summary (summary_2), a contiguous multi-token full-name literal that explicitly identifies or is explicitly linked to the same person (for example via an identity link or biographical framing in summary_2), prefer and emit that multi-token full-name literal exactly as it appears in summary_2. Do not prefer the longer form unless summary_2 provides an explicit identity link tying the alias to the full-name literal; if no such explicit link is present, follow the base identity-link rules and do not force selection of the longer form.

When in doubt about whether the summaries license a single definitive literal, prefer to emit uncertainty only if the required supporting premise or identity link is actually missing, ambiguous, or conflicting. Do not make uncertainty the default when composition or transitive reasoning is required and the necessary premises are present.

Ensure the `answer` field contains only the minimal answer string supported by the summaries (subject to the formatting rule above).

response.hop1_query: Why was O.J Mayo, named the Most Valuable Player at the 2007 Anaheim Classic dismissed from the NBA?
hop1_docs: 2007 Anaheim Classic | The 2007 Anaheim Classic was played between November 22 and November 25, 2007 at the Anaheim Convention Center in Anaheim, California.  The champion of the tournament was Southern California, who defeated Southern Illinois in the Championship Game.  The Most Valuable Player was O. J. Mayo of Southern California.

2013 NBA Summer League | The 2013 NBA Summer League is a pro basketball league run by the NBA just after the 2013 NBA draft.  It gives newly drafted players a chance to test their skills against each other, and to give them a feel for professional basketball.  All 30 NBA teams participated, along with the D-League Select.  The Miami Heat were the only team to participate in both Summer Leagues.  It ran from July 7–12 in Orlando and July 12–22 in Las Vegas.  Jeremy Lamb of the Oklahoma City Thunder was named the Most Valuable Player of the Orlando Summer League.  Jonas Valančiūnas of the Toronto Raptors went on to be named the Most Valuable Player of the Las Vegas Summer League.  Ian Clark of the Golden State Warriors was named the Most Valuable Player of the Las Vegas Summer League Championship Game.

Kevin Durant | Kevin Wayne Durant (born September 29, 1988) is an American professional basketball player for the Golden State Warriors of the National Basketball Association (NBA).  He has won an NBA championship, an NBA Most Valuable Player Award, the Bill Russell NBA Finals Most Valuable Player Award, the NBA All-Star Game Most Valuable Player Award, four NBA scoring titles, the NBA Rookie of the Year Award, and two Olympic gold medals.  Durant has also been selected to seven All-NBA teams and eight NBA All-Star teams.
Bill Russell NBA Finals Most Valuable Player Award | Bill Russell NBA Finals Most Valuable Player Award
NBA Development League Finals Most Valuable Player Award | NBA Development League Finals Most Valuable Player Award

O. J. Mayo | Ovinton J'Anthony "O. J." Mayo (born November 5, 1987) is an American professional basketball player.  He played one season of college basketball for the USC Trojans while earning first-team All-Pac-10 honors.  The team forfeited all of its wins that season and Mayo lost his remaining three years of National Collegiate Athletic Association (NCAA) eligibility after it was ruled he received improper benefits.  Mayo entered the 2008 NBA draft and was selected by the Minnesota Timberwolves with the third overall pick.  He was later traded to the Memphis Grizzlies, with whom he played four seasons.  In 2011, he tested positive for a banned steroid and was suspended by the NBA for 10 games.  Mayo signed with the Dallas Mavericks in 2012, and then with the Milwaukee Bucks in 2013.  On July 1, 2016, Mayo was dismissed from the NBA for violating the league's anti-drug program.

Russell Westbrook | Russell Westbrook III (born November 12, 1988) is an American professional basketball player for the Oklahoma City Thunder of the National Basketball Association (NBA).  He is a six-time NBA All-Star, and a two-time NBA All-Star Game Most Valuable Player, winning consecutive awards in 2015 and 2016.  He is also a six-time All-NBA Team member and led the league in scoring in 2014–15 and 2016–17.  In 2017, Westbrook became one of two players in NBA history to average a triple-double for a season, along with Oscar Robertson in 1962.  He also set a record for the most triple-doubles in a season, with 42.  He was subsequently named the 2016–17 NBA Most Valuable Player.

response.summary_1: Evidence: passage [1] says "The Most Valuable Player was O. J. Mayo of Southern California" (2007 Anaheim Classic). Passage [6] states "On July 1, 2016, Mayo was dismissed from the NBA for violating the league's anti-drug program." Bridge/missing-fact signal: these two passages link O. J. Mayo (the 2007 Anaheim Classic MVP) to his NBA dismissal; no additional identity link is needed. Use the quoted phrases above as retrieval anchors.
response.hop2_query: O. J. Mayo dismissed NBA July 1 2016 anti-drug program violation
hop2_docs: O. J. Mayo | Ovinton J'Anthony "O. J." Mayo (born November 5, 1987) is an American professional basketball player.  He played one season of college basketball for the USC Trojans while earning first-team All-Pac-10 honors.  The team forfeited all of its wins that season and Mayo lost his remaining three years of National Collegiate Athletic Association (NCAA) eligibility after it was ruled he received improper benefits.  Mayo entered the 2008 NBA draft and was selected by the Minnesota Timberwolves with the third overall pick.  He was later traded to the Memphis Grizzlies, with whom he played four seasons.  In 2011, he tested positive for a banned steroid and was suspended by the NBA for 10 games.  Mayo signed with the Dallas Mavericks in 2012, and then with the Milwaukee Bucks in 2013.  On July 1, 2016, Mayo was dismissed from the NBA for violating the league's anti-drug program.

Eduard Vorganov | Eduard Vorganov (born 7 December 1982) is a Russian professional road bicycle racer who rides for team Minsk Cycling Club .  In June 2012, Vorganov won the Russian Road Race Championships.  On 5 February 2016, it was announced that he was suspended for a positive test to meldonium conducted on 14 January.  However, in May of that year he was absolved of the doping violation when the World Anti-Doping Agency offered an amnesty to sportspeople who tested positive for the drug in the early months of the year, on the basis that traces of the drug could have remained in athletes' systems from taking it before the ban on meldonium was introduced.  In December 2016 it was announced that Vorganov would join the Minsk Cycling Club team for 2017.

High Intensity Drug Trafficking Area | The High Intensity Drug Trafficking Area program (HIDTA) is a drug-prohibition enforcement program run by the United States Office of National Drug Control Policy.  It was established in 1990 after the Anti-Drug Abuse Act of 1988 was passed.

Chris Andersen | Christopher Claus Andersen (born July 7, 1978) is an American professional basketball player who last played for the Cleveland Cavaliers of the National Basketball Association (NBA).  Nicknamed "Birdman", Andersen was born in Long Beach, California, grew up in Iola, Texas, and played one year at Blinn College.  Andersen began his professional career in the Chinese Basketball Association and the American minor leagues.  He then played in the NBA for the Denver Nuggets and the New Orleans Hornets.  He received a two-year ban from the NBA in 2006 for violating the league's drug policy, but was reinstated on March 4, 2008, and re-signed with the Hornets the next day.  He returned to Denver later in 2008, and remained with the team until 2012.  He signed with the Miami Heat in January 2013 and won a championship with them that same year.  He is the only Blinn student to ever play in the NBA.

Office of Criminal Investigations | The Office of Criminal Investigations (OCI) was established to provide the US Food and Drug Administration (FDA) with a specific Office to conduct and coordinate Criminal investigations. OCI special agents employ customary federal law enforcement methods and techniques in the investigation of suspected criminal violations of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act, the Federal Anti-Tampering Act, and other related federal statutes. OCI investigations concentrate on significant violations of these laws, with a priority on conduct that may present a danger to the public health.  OCI is a relatively small agency, employing approximately 180 Special Agents.

Organized Crime Drug Enforcement Task Force | The Organized Crime Drug Enforcement Task Force is a federal drug enforcement program in the United States, overseen by the Attorney General and the Department of Justice.  It primarily concerns itself with the disruption of major drug trafficking operations and related crimes, such as money laundering, tax and weapon violations, and violent crime.  The task force was created in 1982, and employs approximately 2500 agents.

Sérgio Henrique Ferreira | Sérgio Henrique Ferreira (October 4, 1934 – July 17, 2016) was a Brazilian physician and pharmacologist noted for the discovery of the bradykinin potentiating factor, which led to new and widely used anti-hypertension drugs — the ACE inhibitors.
hop2_titles: Organized Crime Drug Enforcement Task Force
response.summary_2: O. J. Mayo, who was the Most Valuable Player at the 2007 Anaheim Classic, was dismissed from the NBA on July 1, 2016 for violating the league's anti-drug program.
response.answer: for violating the league's anti-drug program
